title: Nvidia’s version of OpenClaw could solve its biggest problem: security
description: Nvidia announced an open enterprise AI agent platform, called NemoClaw, that is built off of viral OpenClaw.

title: Jensen Huang just put Nvidia’s Blackwell and Vera Rubin sales projections into the $1 trillion stratosphere
description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said he expects $1 trillion worth of orders for the chips.

title: Warren presses Pentagon over decision to grant xAI access to classified networks
description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren noted that Grok, xAI's controversial chatbot, has created harmful outputs for users and poses a potential national security risk.

title: Memories AI is building the visual memory layer for wearables and robotics
description: Memories.ai is building a large visual memory model that can index and retrieve video-recorded memories for physical AI.

title: Elon Musk’s xAI faces child porn lawsuit from minors Grok allegedly undressed
description: The three plaintiffs are seeking to represent anyone who had real images of them as a minor altered into sexual content by Grok.

title: Nvidia’s DLSS 5 uses generative AI to boost photorealism in video games, with ambitions beyond gaming
description: Nvidia’s new DLSS 5 uses generative AI and structured graphics data to make video games more realistic. CEO Jensen Huang says the approach could eventually spread to other industries.

title: How to watch Jensen Huang’s Nvidia GTC 2026 keynote — and what to expect
description: GTC is Nvidia's flagship annual event, where the chipmaker typically announces new products, partnerships, and its vision for the future of computing. Huang's keynote will focus on Nvidia's role in the future of computing and AI.

title: Another deep tech chip startup becomes a unicorn: Frore hits $1.64B
description: At Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's urging, Frore developed liquid-cooling tech for chips. That shift helped it raise $143 million.

title: Fuse raises $25M  to disrupt aging loan origination systems used by US credit unions
description: The startup also announced a $5 million "rescue fund" to help credit unions ditch legacy software for its AI-native platform.

title: The dictionary sues OpenAI
description: Encyclopedia Britannica and Merriam-Webster say that OpenAI violated the copyright of almost 100,000 articles by using them for LLM training.

title: Google, Accel India accelerator chooses 5 startups and none are ‘AI wrappers’
description: Google and Accel say about 70% of AI startup pitches tied to India were "wrappers" as they reviewed more than 4,000 applications for their Atoms cohort.

title: ByteDance reportedly pauses global launch of its Seedance 2.0 video generator
description: The company is reportedly delaying the launch as its engineers and lawyers work to avert further legal issues.

title: Lawyer behind AI psychosis cases warns of mass casualty risks
description: AI chatbots have been linked to suicides for years. Now one lawyer says they are showing up in mass casualty cases too, and the technology is moving faster than the safeguards.

title: Wiz investor unpacks Google’s $32B acquisition
description: Shardul Shah of Index Ventures walks us through Google's biggest acquisition ever.

title: US Army announces contract with Anduril worth up to $20B
description: The Army described this as a single enterprise contract consolidating more than 120 separate "procurement actions."

title: Meta reportedly considering layoffs that could affect 20% of the company
description: These layoffs could help Facebook's parent company offset its aggressive spending on AI infrastructure, as well as AI-related acquisitions and hiring.

title: How to use the new ChatGPT app integrations, including DoorDash, Spotify, Uber, and others
description: Learn how to use Spotify, Canva, Figma, Expedia, and other apps directly in ChatGPT.

title: ‘Not built right the first time’ — Musk’s xAI is starting over again, again
description: The AI lab is revamping its effort to build an AI coding tool, with two new executives joining from Cursor.

title: Nyne, founded by a father-son duo, gives AI agents the human context they’re missing
description: The data infrastructure startup raised $5.3 million in seed funding led by Wischoff Ventures and South Park Commons.

title: Steven Spielberg says he’s ‘never used AI’ in any of his films
description: At SXSW, Steven Spielberg said AI has uses in many fields, but not when it comes to replacing creative people in film and TV writing.
